# Rondo

> Scripted, multi-provider AI/LLM dispatch with verified execution and a
> cross-vendor jury: the model that writes the code does not certify it — a
> *different* vendor independently reviews the result, and disagreements are the
> signal. Drives a coding AI in a loop, runs the tests and hashes the files
> itself (the model's "passed" is never the gate), then convenes the jury.

Rondo routes prompts to Claude, Gemini, Grok, Mistral, OpenAI, the Anthropic API,
and Ollama from MCP (Claude Code), a CLI, or a Python API — with budgets, a
uniform error envelope, an audit trail, secret sanitization, and repeatable
output contracts. Think "Terraform for prompts." It is maintainer-mediated and
source-available (see CONTRIBUTING.md).

## Key docs
- [The cross-vendor jury (the thesis)](docs/CROSS-VENDOR-JURY.md): why a competitor's model vetoing the author's model is the moat single-vendor tools structurally won't build.
- [Getting started](docs/GETTING-STARTED.md): install and first run.
- [The Golden Five](docs/GOLDEN-FIVE.md): a stranger's first hour, five commands.
- [How Rondo scores itself](docs/SCORING.md): the blind cross-vendor hostile-review trajectory (3.13 → 5.3) and the honest reliability split.
- [API stability](docs/API-STABILITY.md): the stable CLI commands, MCP tools, and config keys.
- [Prompt coding](docs/PROMPT-CODING.md): looping small verified steps to control a coding AI.

## Examples
- [controlled_review_loop.py](examples/api/controlled_review_loop.py): live — Claude builds, Gemini + Grok judge, a bug that passes its own tests gets caught.

## Topics
llm, ai, multi-provider, cross-vendor, ai-code-review, code-review, llm-jury,
claude, gemini, grok, mistral, openai, ollama, mcp, agents, loop-engineering,
verified-execution, dispatch, prompt-engineering, python, cli
